system.docHtml: Tracking is the art of reading the subtle signs left by the passage of people and animals through the environment—footprints, broken vegetation, disturbed soil, scent trails, and other physical evidence of movement. A skilled tracker can determine the number, direction, speed, and approximate time of passage of their quarry, and may identify specific individuals or species from their distinctive sign. The difficulty varies enormously with terrain and weather conditions, as hard ground and rain quickly erase the traces that soft earth and still air preserve.
